title: Global backbone: Yggdrasil integration for inter-mesh routing
description:
## Vision

Any LICHEN node can reach any other LICHEN node worldwide. Gateways run Yggdrasil, forming a global backbone. No central authority, no RIR bureaucracy, cryptographic addressing.

## Architecture

```
[Portland mesh]          [Berlin mesh]           [Tokyo mesh]
     │                        │                       │
  Gateway A               Gateway B               Gateway C
     │                        │                       │
     └──────── Yggdrasil network (0200::/7) ──────────┘
                              │
                    Auto-peering, self-healing
                    DHT-based routing
```

## Why Yggdrasil

| Feature | Benefit for LICHEN |
|---------|-------------------|
| Crypto addresses | Derived from Ed25519 keys — we already have these! |
| No central authority | No registry, no RIR, no single point of failure |
| Self-healing | Finds paths automatically, survives gateway failures |
| Works over anything | Internet, Tailscale, direct peering, whatever |
| Battle-tested | Running since 2018, active community |
| Open source | MIT license, Go implementation |

## Address mapping

### Option 1: Derive Yggdrasil address from LICHEN identity
- LICHEN uses Ed25519 keypairs
- Yggdrasil uses Ed25519 for addressing
- Same key → same address in both systems
- Gateway advertises mesh prefix as Yggdrasil subnet

### Option 2: Separate Yggdrasil identity for gateway
- Gateway has its own Yggdrasil keypair
- Mesh nodes have ULA addresses
- Gateway NATs/proxies between mesh and Yggdrasil
- Simpler but less elegant

**Recommendation: Option 1** — unified identity

## Traffic flow

### Mesh A node → Mesh B node
1. Node A sends to Node B's LICHEN address
2. Gateway A: not in my mesh → forward to Yggdrasil
3. Yggdrasil routes to Gateway B
4. Gateway B: in my mesh → forward via LoRa
5. Node B receives

### Discovery
- No explicit discovery needed
- Yggdrasil DHT handles routing
- Gateway just needs to peer with Yggdrasil network

## Gateway requirements

- Run yggdrasil daemon (or embed library)
- Peer with public Yggdrasil nodes (or private peers)
- Advertise mesh subnet as Yggdrasil route
- Forward between LoRa and Yggdrasil interfaces

## Addressing scheme

```
LICHEN mesh-internal:  fd00:lichen:<mesh-id>::<node-iid>
LICHEN via Yggdrasil:  02xx:xxxx:...::<node-iid>
                       └── derived from Ed25519 pubkey
```

Both addresses work. Internal uses ULA. Global uses Yggdrasil.

## Benefits over alternatives

| Alternative | Problem |
|-------------|---------|
| RIR allocation | Bureaucracy, cost, single org controls it |
| Tailscale | Requires account, not fully decentralized |
| Central registry | Single point of failure, trust issues |
| Just ULA | Not globally routable |

## Integration with previous work

- Border router epic (p2y5): add Yggdrasil as backhaul option
- Multi-gateway (mugl): gateways discover each other via Yggdrasil
- Gateway-centric mode (dk3c): still applies, just global now
notes: Updated gateway impl notes for pass3: Yggdrasil address derivation from Ed25519 pubkey uses SHA-512( pubkey )[0..8] with prefix 0x02 for the /7 range per Yggdrasil spec, ensuring Rust (lichen-core::addr::NodeId::ygg_addr_from_pubkey) and C (lichen_eui_from_ed25519 in link/identity) produce identical IIDs. Dropped ULA entirely. Alignment verified against test/vectors/yggdrasil.json (to be added). Routing prefers local RPL match before Yggdrasil TUN forward. 3 codereview passes pending.
